title: gaultheria venusta
intro: Gaultheria venusta, also known as the New Zealand wintergreen, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia. It is a small, evergreen shrub that belongs to the heath family and is known for its red berries and fragrant leaves. The leaves of Gaultheria venusta contain methyl salicylate, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, and it can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil for topical use.
